name: Canada
contextBlurb: As of 2025, Canada has no dedicated stablecoin statute. Most fiat-referenced “stablecoins” (VRCAs) are addressed through CSA securities/derivatives oversight of trading platforms under an interim approach, not an issuer law. The Bank of Canada has urged a payments-focused framework so that any widely used stablecoin meets prudential and consumer-protection standards. Deposit insurance does not cover crypto/stablecoins, so retail users lack bank-style protections.

legislation.title: FINTRAC fines Cryptomus operator Xeltox Enterprises Ltd. a record C$176.96 million for AML and sanctions violations, signaling strict enforcement in the crypto sector.
The U.S. GENIUS Act is implemented, creating a clear federal framework for stablecoin regulation, increasing pressure on Canada to harmonize its approach.
CSA issues a decision document on Payward Canada, reinforcing the application of securities laws to crypto platforms and stablecoin trading.

news.headline: CSA — Interim approach to Value-Referenced Crypto Assets (stablecoins) (press release / Staff Notice links)
CSA — Update on VRCAs for crypto trading platforms (2024)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: As of 2025, Canada has no dedicated stablecoin statute. Most fiat-referenced “stablecoins” (VRCAs) are addressed through CSA securities/derivatives oversight of trading platforms under an interim approach, not an issuer law. The Bank of Canada has urged a payments-focused framework so that any widely used stablecoin meets prudential and consumer-protection standards. Deposit insurance does not cover crypto/stablecoins, so retail users lack bank-style protections.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Canada has no dedicated issuer law. The CSA treats fiat-pegged stablecoins on trading platforms as “Value-Referenced Crypto Assets (VRCAs)” and generally securities and/or derivatives in the platform context.
